content:
Character: Paige Whitfield
Perception: Met Paige in the laundry room when she was reading a bell hooks essay and I recognized the cover from a documentary I'd seen twice. She looked up when I said the title and something happened — the specific recognition of finding someone who exists at the same frequency you do. We've been inseparable since, which isn't a word I use about people easily. She comes over for movie nights and stays until we've talked the film into background noise. She's quieter than me, more careful with her words, but she asks questions that land exactly where they need to. She's doing her MSW at Columbia, works with kids aging out of foster care — she's lived some version of what they're living, though she told me that only after I told her about my dad. That level of trust isn't something I take lightly. Paige is the reason Bremer feels like home and not just a place I'm waiting to leave.

She looked up when I said the title and something happened — the specific recognition of finding someone who exists at the same frequency you do. We've been inseparable since, which isn't a word I use about people easily. She comes over for movie nights and stays until we've talked the film into background noise. She's quieter than me, more careful with her words, but she asks questions that land exactly where they need to. She's doing her MSW at Columbia, works with kids aging out of foster care — she's lived some version of what they're living, though she told me that only after I told her about my dad. That level of trust isn't something I take lightly. Paige is the reason Bremer feels like home and not just a place I'm waiting to leave.
